words.def: the fact of owning something
words.example: a growth in home ownership

words.def: a chemical element. Oxygen is a gas that is present in air and water and is necessary for people, animals and plants to live.
words.example: The patient didn't seem to be getting enough oxygen.

words.def: the speed at which somebody/something walks, runs or moves
words.example: at a… pace, to set off at a steady/gentle/leisurely pace

words.def: something that is wrapped in paper or put into a thick envelope so that it can be sent by mail, carried easily, or given as a present
words.example: A large package has arrived for you.

words.def: a small container made of paper or card in which goods are packed for selling
words.example: a packet of biscuits/cigarettes/crisps

words.def: a straight tree with a mass of long leaves at the top, growing in tropical countries. There are several types of palm tree, some of which produce fruit.
words.example: a date palm

words.def: a square or rectangular piece of wood, glass or metal that forms part of a larger surface such as a door or wall
words.example: One of the glass panels in the front door was cracked.

words.def: a sudden feeling of great fear that cannot be controlled and prevents you from thinking clearly
words.example: a moment of panic

words.def: a public celebration of a special day or event, usually with bands in the streets and decorated vehicles
words.example: the Lord Mayor’s parade

words.def: two or more lines that are parallel to each other are the same distance apart at every point
words.example: parallel lines

words.def: the group of people who are elected to make and change the laws of a country
words.example: The German parliament is called the ‘Bundestag’.

words.def: for part of the day or week in which people work
words.example: She's looking for a part-time job.

words.def: a person who is taking part in an activity or event
words.example: The average age of study participants was 48 years.

words.def: the act of taking part in an activity or event
words.example: a show with lots of audience participation

words.def: used to emphasize that you are referring to one individual person, thing or type of thing and not others
words.example: There is one particular patient I'd like you to see.

words.def: to some extent; not completely
words.example: Some people are unwilling to attend the classes partly because of the cost involved.

words.def: the state of being a partner in business
words.example: to be in/to go into partnership

words.def: a long narrow area with walls on either side that connects one room or place with another
words.example: A dark narrow passage led to the main hall.

words.def: having or showing strong feelings of sexual love or of anger, etc.
words.example: to have a passionate nature

words.def: a series of letters, numbers, etc. that you must type into a computer or computer system in order to be able to use it
words.example: Enter a username and password to get into the system.

words.def: the ability to stay calm and accept a delay or something annoying without complaining
words.example: She has little patience with (= will not accept or consider) such views.

words.def: able to wait for a long time or accept annoying behaviour or difficulties without becoming angry
words.example: You'll just have to be patient and wait till I'm finished.

words.def: a period of time during which somebody stops talking or stops what they are doing
words.example: There was a long pause before she answered.

words.def: the act of paying somebody/something or of being paid
words.example: What method of payment do you prefer?

words.def: a person who is the same age or who has the same social status as you
words.example: She enjoys the respect of her peers.

words.def: a punishment for breaking a law, rule or contract
words.example: to impose a penalty

words.def: to understand or think of somebody/something in a particular way
words.example: perceive somebody/something/yourself (as something), This discovery was perceived as a major breakthrough.

words.def: the number, amount or rate of something, expressed as if it is part of a total that is 100; a part or share of a whole
words.example: percentage of something/somebody, What percentage of the population is/are overweight?

words.def: an idea, a belief or an image you have as a result of how you see or understand something
words.example: a campaign to change public perception of the police

words.def: lasting for a long time or for all time in the future; existing all the time
words.example: She was unable to find a permanent job.

words.def: in a way that lasts for a long time or for all time in the future; in a way that exists all the time
words.example: The stroke left his right side permanently damaged.

words.def: an official document that gives somebody the right to do something, especially for a limited period of time
words.example: a parking/building permit

words.def: a particular attitude towards something; a way of thinking about something
words.example: a global perspective

words.def: a stage in a process of change or development
words.example: during the initial/final phase

words.def: a fact or an event in nature or society, especially one that is not fully understood
words.example: cultural/natural phenomena

words.def: the study of the nature and meaning of the universe and of human life
words.example: philosophy of something, the philosophy of science

words.def: an act of choosing something
words.example: Take your pick (= choose).

words.def: a painting or drawing, etc. that shows a scene, a person or thing
words.example: He likes books with lots of pictures in them.

words.def: a number of things that have been placed on top of each other
words.example: pile of something, a pile of clothes/paper

words.def: a small flat round piece of medicine that you swallow whole, without biting it
words.example: a vitamin pill

words.def: a short thin piece of stiff wire with a sharp point at one end and a round head at the other, used especially for fastening together pieces of cloth when sewing
words.example: Use pins to keep the patch in place while you sew it on.

words.def: an area of ground specially prepared and marked for playing a sports game
words.example: a football pitch

words.def: used to show that you are disappointed about something
words.example: a pity (that…), It's a pity that you can't stay longer.

words.def: the act of finding somebody a suitable job or place to live
words.example: a job placement service

words.def: not decorated or complicated; simple
words.example: a plain but elegant dress

words.def: the act or process of making plans for something
words.example: The department is responsible for all financial planning.

words.def: the series of events that form the story of a novel, play, film, etc.
words.example: It's hard to follow the plot of the film.
